# Focus Flow

## Intent
Reduce simultaneous load and present one meaningful chunk at a time.

## Best For
- Learners who benefit from fewer competing elements
- ADHD-friendly and dyslexia-sensitive support
- Scaffolded classroom or intervention settings

## Not Ideal For
- Dense comparison lessons
- Topics that require multiple simultaneous visuals

## Learner Fit
- ADHD-friendly
- Dyslexia-sensitive
- Scaffolded

## Subjects
- Mathematics
- Science
- English

## Lesson Flow
Hook -> Short explain -> Support cue -> Practice -> Reflect -> What Next

## Required Components
- SectionHeader
- HookHero
- ExplanationBlock
- PracticeStack
- WhatNextBridge

## Optional Components
- DefinitionCard
- GlossaryInline
- PitfallAlert
- ReflectionPrompt

## Why This Template Exists
Accommodation is not only a color choice. This template lowers cognitive load structurally by removing simultaneous competition on the page.

## Responsive Notes
Keep the same single-column pacing on every breakpoint and avoid introducing sidebars at larger sizes.

## Print Notes
Maintain the same chunked order in print and preserve flat-list practice spacing.
